title: Manager’s messy notes
subtitle: Turn a rushed update into an Aiko-ready summary.
storyContext: Aiko needs a clean update. Ashley sent a rushed note. Your job is to make it Aiko-ready.
instructions: Define three buckets: Split the content into exactly three distinct categories, each capturing a clear theme or purpose.
Start with BSP: Begin every bullet point with a bolded summary phrase (BSP) that is short and descriptive.
sourceText:
Hey, quick update on the McDonald’s launch.

We are launching a limited-time McDonald’s x Pokémon Happy Meal in the US. This is new because we are launching 8 blind box collectibles with 8 designs, box art that can be cut out, and a QR code that unlocks a Pokémon Trainer Certification once customers collect all 8 boxes.

We aim to market this by using in-app push notifications, billboards, and partnering with known collectors to drive hype.

Key issues include a limit of 2 boxes per person to prevent hoarding and logistics risk from under-supply.

— Ashley Kat, VP of Partnerships
checklist: Do my sections cover the full message?
Does each bullet start with a BSP?
Did I keep the answer concise?

sampleAnswer.title: What will we launch?
bullets: Blind-box collectibles: Launch 8 collectible designs to drive repeat visits.
Cut-out box art: Make packaging part of the collectible experience.
Trainer certification: Use a QR journey to reward customers who collect all 8 boxes.

sampleAnswer.title: How will we launch?
bullets: In-app pushes: Use targeted prompts to drive store visits.
Billboards: Build mass awareness for the limited-time launch.
Collector partners: Create credibility and hype through known collectors.

sampleAnswer.title: What could break?
bullets: Purchase limits: Cap purchases at 2 boxes per person to reduce hoarding.
Under-supply risk: Manage logistics carefully so scarcity does not damage momentum.

prompt: Turn the messy notes into a structured response.
